channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: As Trump nominee battles brew, NC Senate cleared of raucous onlookers
item.description: Lt. Gov. Mark Robinson cleared the North Carolina Senate chamber this week for the second time in recent days amid protestations against the override of Gov. Cooper's veto on an election board appointments law.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump in Paris for Notre Dame Cathedral's reopening and to meet with Macron
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Donald Trump is off on his first international trip as president-elect, ready to join world leaders and dignitaries in Paris for a Saturday celebration of the renovation of Notre Dame Cathedral after a devastating fire in 2019.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET and ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Election Day has long passed. In some states, legislatures are working to undermine the results
item.description: Voters in some parts of the country are discovering that having their say at the ballot box is not necessarily the final word, even though this year's election was over a month ago.
item.dc:creator: Gary Fields, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The DNC chair candidates discuss Joe Biden, Kamala Harris and winning Latino voters
item.description: Four Democrats are running for Democratic National Committee chair, looking to take on the task of reinvigorating a party demoralized by a second loss to President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan J. Cooper, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Bureau of Prisons agrees to court monitor, public acknowledgement of staff-on-inmate sexual abuse
item.description: A legal settlement in the wake of rampant staff-on-inmate sexual abuse will force the federal Bureau of Prisons to open its doors to a court-appointed monitor and publicly acknowledge pervasive misconduct at its now-shuttered women’s prison in California.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak And Michael Balsamo, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Congressman Torres blasts crime in NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The cold-blooded shooting of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson in the heart of Manhattan shocked New Yorkers, including Congressman Ritchie Torres, leaving the lawmaker to pose the question: “What is happening in New York City?” Torres joined PIX on Politics to discuss city crim
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: How China's cyberespionage has changed
item.description: China's cyberespionage has evolved over the last several decades from targeting entities such as government departments to the public tech sector.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump offers a public show of support for Hegseth and says he believes he can be confirmed
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump on Friday offered a public show of support for Pete Hegseth, his embattled choice to lead the Defense Department, whose confirmation by the Senate is in doubt as he faces questions over allegations of excessive drinking, sexual assault and his views on
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG and JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump taps forceful ally of hard-line immigration policies to head Customs and Border Protection
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The picture of who will be in charge of executing President-elect Donald Trump's hard-line immigration and border policies has come into sharper focus after he announced his picks to head Customs and Border Protection and also the agency tasked with deporting immigrants in the coun
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Top Midwestern university becomes latest college to roll back DEI initiatives as trend goes national
item.description: The University of Michigan became the latest institution to drop DEI requirements, citing risks to free speech and intellectual diversity on campus.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden weighs preemptive pardons before leaving office
item.description: President Joe Biden is weighing sweeping pardons for allies, who the White House believes could be targeted by President-elect Donald Trump’s incoming administration, according to people familiar with the matter.
item.dc:creator: Vinay Simlot, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: GOP senator announces 'DOGE Acts' to back Musk, Ramaswamy government cost-cutting objectives
item.description: Blackburn announced she will be introducing the "DOGE Acts," following meetings with Musk and Ramaswamy on Capitol Hill on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Newsom visits border, slams Trump on planned deportations, tariffs
item.description: "No state will be more impacted by that, no region will be impacted by that, small businesses that will lose customers, jobs that will be lost, inflationary impacts on every single one of you," said Newsom.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: Melania Trump says heading to the White House for the second term is much different than the first
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Soon-to-be first lady Melania Trump said Friday that preparing to enter the White House for the second time looks a lot different from the first go-around. Now, she and her husband know what to expect.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mayor Adams on City of Yes plan passing City Council vote
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The New York City Council has approved Mayor Eric Adams' ambitious City of Yes plan. The $5 billion plan will rezone the Big Apple for the first time in six decades. It's estimated to create more than 80,000 new housing units over 15 years.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Elon Musk funded a super PAC comparing Trump's position on abortion to Ruth Bader Ginsburg's
item.description: Elon Musk was the sole funder of a super PAC formed less than a month before the election that focused on advertising intended to convince voters that Donald Trump 's stance on abortion was akin to that of late Supreme Court Justice Ruth Bader Ginsburg.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump names former Sen. David Perdue of Georgia to be ambassador to China
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump has selected former Sen. David Perdue of Georgia to be the U.S. ambassador to China, leaning on a former business executive turned politician to serve as the administration's envoy to America's most potent economic and military adversary.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Five new details in search for UnitedHealthcare CEO's shooter
item.description: New details emerged on Thursday as the manhunt entered its second day for the suspected gunman in the fatal shooting of United Healthcare CEO Brian Thompson.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hegseth faces senators' concerns not only about his behavior but also his views on women in combat
item.description: Minnesotan Pete Hegseth has spent the week on Capitol Hill trying to reassure Republican senators that he is fit to lead President-elect Donald Trump’s Department of Defense in the wake of high-profile allegations about excessive drinking and sexual assault.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Kevin McCarthy: Gaetz lied to Trump before AG nomination
item.description: Should the House Ethics Committee still release its report on Gaetz, even if he’s not going to be part of Trump’s Cabinet?
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Steven Horsford to hand off role as chair of Congressional Black Caucus
item.description: Nevada Congressman Steven Horsford will no longer be the chair of the Congressional Black Caucus for the 119th Congress.
item.dc:creator: Linsey Lewis, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Fani Willis ordered to turn over communications in Trump case to conservative group
item.description: A Georgia judge ordered Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis to turn over to a conservative watchdog group any communications she may have with special counsel Jack Smith or the now-disbanded House Jan. 6 committee.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Trump nominates Paul Atkins as SEC chair
item.description: “He also recognizes that digital assets & other innovations are crucial to Making America Greater than Ever Before,” the president-elect added.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Las Vegas Mayor Shelley Berkley sworn into office; envisions future of prosperity for the city
item.description: Shelley Berkley was sworn in as the mayor of Las Vegas on Wednesday, promising to serve the city well and work with the community to create an atmosphere of growth and prosperity.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Bleakley, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Montana lawmakers cross the aisle to block a trans bathroom ban in the state capitol
item.description: Some Republicans joined Democrats in rejecting the measure, which would have restricted bathroom access. Rep. Zooey Zephyr, Montana's first openly transgender legislator, won a second term last month.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman, MPR News

item.title: Pete Hegseth’s mother says The New York Times made ‘threats’ by asking her to comment on a story
item.description: A basic tenet of journalism — calling someone for comment on a story — was seen as a threat by defense secretary nominee Pete Hegseth’s mother.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Ocasio-Cortez first person to hit a million followers on Bluesky
item.description: The platform’s features are based off of Twitter, and they include the ability to message fellow users as well as a discover and feed tab.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Trump appointments set off special elections to fill House seats: What to know
item.description: The move by President-elect Trump to tap several House Republicans to fill positions in his Cabinet and beyond has set off a fresh round of special elections next year to replace them. Trump chose Reps. Mike Waltz (R-Fla.) and Elise Stefanik (R-N.Y.) to be his national security adviser and U.S.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, Fox 5

item.title: Trump falls just below 50% in popular vote, but gets more than in past elections
item.description: This year's popular-vote margin is the second-closest since 1968 and still tightening. It shows just how closely divided the country is politically, and that any shift to the right is marginal.
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro, MPR News

item.title: California Assembly Speaker sets new bill limit for legislators
item.description: The California Assembly will limit the number of bills each legislature can introduce from 50 to 35, according to the house rules adopted at the start of the session on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: GOP's small majority adds to challenge of tax reform
item.description: House Republicans are heading into the new Congress with a lofty set of tax reform goals, an ambitious agenda made more difficult by a razor-thin majority.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, Fox 5

item.title: Trump nominates Kash Patel to lead FBI
item.description: President-elect Trump plans to nominate Kash Patel to serve as head of the FBI, elevating a staunch ally and controversial figure who sparked internal battles during the first Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WFLA, NBC
